rulings.Case Title: SUSAN BURGESS ET AL VS. PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Unopposed Motion For An Order Sealing The Amount And Terms Of Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for July 23, 2025, line 1. Plaintiff's Unopposed Motion for an Order Sealing the Amount and Terms of Settlement is DENIED. Plaintiff's motion relies on conjecture. The Court must make "express factual findings" to seal records, yet Plaintiff's attached declaration contains no facts in support of the requirements of sealing (See CRC 2.550(d)). Further, Plaintiff does not explain how settlement amounts are "commercial information" over which corporations retain a privacy interest. Nor does Plaintiff explain how or why PG&E's competitors - who operate in a highly abnormal market - would make use of PG&E's settlement amounts. =(501/VMH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tent ative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE FULLER VS. SHELLPOINT MTG, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint For Vilations Of Civ. Code 2923.6 (Failure To Provide Acknowledgment, Assignment Of Single Person, Failure To Notify Foreclosure - Prevention, Dual Tracking) And Negligence; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for July 23, 2025, line 2. Plaintiff's Motion for Leave to Amend Complaint for Violations of Civ. Code 2923.6 is OFF CALENDAR. Invalid Proof of Service on file. POS was executed on June 15, 2025, attesting to service for the following day, June 16, 2025. rulings.Case Title: KURT GRIMES VS. RUSHMORE LOAN MANAGEMENT SERVICES, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for July 23, 2025, line 3. Defendant's Demurrer to Amended Complaint is OFF CALENDAR. Plaintiff is ordered to meet and confer in good faith to address the issues raised by the demurrer. Additionally, the demurrer was filed May 23, 2025 and set for hearing on July 23, 2025. "Demurrers must be set for hearing not more than 35 days following the filing of the demurrer or on the first date available to the court thereafter. For good cause shown, the court may order the hearing held on an earlier or later day on notice prescribed by the court." (CRC 3.1320(d)). Defendant did not provide an explanation for the selected hearing date. =(501/VMH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tent ative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: PRESTON MARKS VS. ELISE YAMAGUCHI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for July 23, 2025, line 4. Defendant's Motion to Quash Service of Summons or Stay or Dismiss is GRANTED. Service of summons is quashed. As this Court already ruled on June 10, 2025 with regard to the OSC Re: Preliminary Injunction, "the Court required Plaintiff to personally serve the Complaint, Summons, and moving papers. Plaintiff's substitute service without a declaration of diligence is thus improper." The Court also finds that the purported service to a Post Box Inc. address in Pacifica was improper. =(501/VMH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: MIN HWA MAC VS. ZHI-JIAN LIANG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for July 23, 2025, line 12. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. The Notice of Termination of Tenancy is fatally defective, because it provides an inaccurate description of the tenants' rights under SFRO 37.9(j). While the full text of this section is attached to the Notice, the language of the Notice itself is inconsistent with that of the attachment. A tenant cannot reasonably be expected to understand that the language in the attachment, and not that in the Notice itself, in fact provides the accurate information. (2710 Sutter Ventures, LLC v. Millis (2022) 82 Cal.App.5th 842, 863.) =(501/VMH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tent ative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
